sources.writer_profile: Alfonso Campos, writing as El Gocho Gourmet, is a Granada-born food critic and hospitality professional who trained at the city's Escuela de Hostelería Hurtado de Mendoza and has worked in several of Granada's most respected restaurants. He is the creator of the El Gocho Gourmet restaurant guide to Granada — now in its second edition with Ayuntamiento backing — and writes a regular gastronomy column for GranadaDigital, Granada's leading independent digital newspaper. His editorial focus centres on authentic, locally rooted establishments rather than tourist-facing venues.

James Dimitri is an independent food and travel writer who visited Granada in February 2025 and documented the city's distinctive free-tapa bar culture with first-hand recommendations. His writing focuses on the authentic everyday dining experience — in particular, the tapas bar tradition that distinguishes Granada from other Spanish cities. His guide reflects the perspective of a knowledgeable, independently minded visitor looking for genuine local bars rather than tourist-facing restaurants.
sources.article_title: How To Eat Well in Granada

sources.writer_profile: Lauren Aloise is an American food writer who studied in Granada and has returned to the city more than twenty times, building deep personal knowledge of its dining scene. She writes the Spanish Sabores food blog and is also co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator active in several Spanish cities. ⚠ coi — her commercial food tour interests should be noted when weighing her restaurant recommendations.
sources.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide
sources.commercial_conflict_note: Lauren Aloise is co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator with active Granada operations. Recommendations may favour tour-compatible venues.

sources.writer_profile: Roberto Ruiz writes for eldiario.es, a national Spanish digital newspaper with strong independent editorial standards. His 2023 article on Granada's essential tapas bars is a focused survey of long-standing Granada classics written from a local journalism perspective, with Spanish-language descriptions for a national Spanish audience.
sources.article_title: De tapas por Granada: siete bares imprescindibles para saborear la ciudad de la Alhambra

restaurants.name: Bar Los Diamantes
restaurants.quote: Open since 1942, specialising in pescadito frito, gambas a la plancha, and champiñones salteados. Cram in for a caña at the Calle Navas or Plaza Nueva location and receive a generous free tapa — Granada's benchmark seafood tapas bar.
restaurants.article_title: How To Eat Well in Granada

restaurants.name: Bodegas Castañeda
restaurants.quote: Meat falling off the bone, collagen broken down, served on a bed of padron peppers and potatoes that had soaked up all the flavour. Go in at aperitif time and you will understand why regulars call it an old-man bar in the best sense.
restaurants.article_title: How To Eat Well in Granada

restaurants.name: Café 4 Gatos
restaurants.quote: Get the toast with tomato, maybe some manchego or jamon on top as well as some good coffee and fresh orange juice. Enormous toasts at a distinctly local spot with options beyond the standard tostada.
restaurants.article_title: How To Eat Well in Granada

restaurants.name: Bar Avila Tapas
restaurants.quote: The star dish is the jamon asado (slow roast pork). It's crispy on the outside, moist in the middle and heavily seasoned — the kind of thing you order again before finishing the first plate.
restaurants.article_title: How To Eat Well in Granada

restaurants.name: Restaurante Más Que Vinos
restaurants.quote: Their wine list is decent and reasonably priced, and their tapas are made using top ingredients.
restaurants.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide

restaurants.name: Tajine Elvira
restaurants.quote: My favorite restaurant for authentic Moroccan food in Granada, this place started as a tiny hole-in-the-wall run by a husband-and-wife team, now in a slightly larger space on Calle Elvira.
restaurants.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide

restaurants.name: Om Kalsum
restaurants.quote: One of the most famous tapas bars in town, here you'll receive a Moroccan-inspired free tapa with every drink.
restaurants.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide

restaurants.name: La Botillería
restaurants.quote: One of the most-loved tapas bars by locals, with great homemade desserts, which is a rarity in Granada tapas bars.
restaurants.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide

restaurants.name: El Trillo
restaurants.quote: For beautifully presented food in a quiet corner of the Albaicín, you can't go wrong at El Trillo — a restaurant (not a tapas bar) with a pretty courtyard on Callejón Aljibe de Trillo; reservation recommended.
restaurants.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide

restaurants.name: La Trastienda
restaurants.quote: This shop/tapas bar is a bit odd — you have to sneak under the bar to get to the back room! A deli at the front specialising in gourmet products and a tiny tapas space behind.
restaurants.article_title: Where to Eat in Granada: The Ultimate Food Guide
